title: Boat Rental in Nosy: 2026 Guide to Choosing the Right Charter
html:
Choosing a boat to rent in Nosy Be, the main departure point for the Mitsio archipelago, typically costs from 250 EUR for a half-day guided excursion, and you can compare verified options on BoatHire24. The island's calm waters and reliable trade winds make it a straightforward decision once you clarify your group size and budget.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Nosy?

Most bareboat charters in Madagascar require a recognised international certificate or a local licence, but skippered boats are licence-free. The country follows international standards, so a Day Skipper or equivalent may be accepted by some operators. Always confirm the exact requirements with the rental company before booking.

Where to set off in Nosy

The main springboard is Nosy Be, a large island off Madagascar's northwest coast. From Hell-Ville, the main port, you can sail to smaller islands such as Nosy Komba, Nosy Sakatia, and Nosy Iranja. Other departure points include the calm anchorage at Ambatoloaka and the marina at Madirokely. Some operators also offer pick-ups from Ankify on the mainland.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat, ideal for a private half-day trip to nearby islands for up to 6 guests, from 250 EUR.
  2. Sailing catamaran, best for a full-day cruise with space for up to 12 guests, from 600 EUR.
  3. Speedboat, perfect for fast transfers or watersports, carrying up to 8 guests, from 350 EUR. You can compare this speedboat on BoatHire24.
  4. Crewed yacht, suited to multi-day explorations, often a 40-foot monohull sleeping up to 8, from 1,200 EUR per day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Nosy?

Prices vary by boat type and duration, but a typical half-day tour starts at around 150 EUR per person.

  • Shared speedboat tour: 3 hours, 150 EUR per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day: 250 EUR for the boat (up to 6 guests).
  • Catamaran full-day: 600 EUR for the boat (up to 12 guests).
  • Crewed yacht full-day: 1,200 EUR (includes skipper and fuel).

To see the full range, you can book a charter online through BoatHire24's listings.

Season and licence

The high season runs from June to October, when the south-east trade wind (the Alize) provides consistent sailing conditions. For licences, bareboat rentals typically require an ICC or equivalent, but skippered options are always available. One local tip worth noting:

If you plan to anchor at Nosy Iranja, arrive before midday to secure a good spot, the sandbar is narrower than it looks on charts and the tide can leave you aground.

At a glance

  • Price band: 150, 1,200 EUR per day
  • Peak months: June to October
  • Typical trip length: 3, 8 hours
  • Guest capacity: 6, 12 persons
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Nosy

Can I rent a boat without prior experience?

Yes, all operators offer skippered charters where a professional captain handles navigation and mooring, so you can relax and enjoy the scenery.

What is the best time of year for a boat trip in Nosy?

The dry season from June to October offers the most stable winds and sunny days, with calm seas ideal for island hopping.

How far is it from Nosy Be to Nosy Iranja?

The distance from Hell-Ville to Nosy Iranja is approximately 30 nautical miles, a journey of about 90 minutes by speedboat.

Are meals provided on full-day charters?

Most catamarans and crewed yachts include a packed lunch or barbecue, but it is best to confirm the catering arrangement when you reserve.

Whether you choose a fast speedboat or a spacious catamaran, planning your rental through a marketplace ensures transparent pricing and verified operators. If you own a boat in this region, you can browse the listings on BoatHire24 to see how listing your vessel works. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Nosy or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Nosy and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
